text:
There once was a quiet girl named Daisy. Daisy loved to read. One day she came across an old book entitled "The Legend of Lucky Linden."

As Daisy read, she couldn't believe how lucky Lucky Linden had been. Lucky had lived in a big old oak tree near her house. Nobody bothered Lucky. Everyone liked animals! Especially big animals.

One day, Lucky fell down. Daisy felt so bad. She climbed up into Lucky's big old branch. There Lucky could hear Daisy.

"Lucky," Daisy said softly.

Just then an owl swooped down.

"Lucky—I mean you—I admire you."

The owl hooted loudly.

"Oh," Daisy giggled.

That night, Daisy built Lucky a new home. A little nest under Lucky. Lucky seemed delighted.

Every day afterwards Daisy went there. They had tea. Lucky loved berries.

So Lucky lived happily ever after. And so did Daisy. Sometimes she'd hear Lucky snuffling around.

And that's why Daisy loved that book.
